channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: NFL tells New England Patriots that team can’t have Bluesky account
item.description: The NFL directed the New England Patriots to delete the team’s Bluesky account, Fred Kirsch, the team’s VP of content, said on a January 16 episode of the Patriots Unfiltered podcast.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon to close 7 warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec and eliminate 1,700 jobs
item.description: Online retailer Amazon said Wednesday that it's closing all seven of its warehouses in the Canadian province of Quebec in the next two months. The e-commerce giant said the move would provide “even more savings to our customers over the long run.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn't in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star. Donaldson stirred interest in a Jan. 13 post on X saying he’d “buy TikTok so it doesn’t get banned.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Your Samsung Galaxy Ring might be the key to a smarter home thanks to SmartThings' connected device roadmap
item.description: At an exclusive VP roundtable during CES, I learned more about Samsung’s plans SmartThings - including how devices like the Samsung Galaxy Ring might fit in
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Quick thinking: snap decisions in the era of real-time analytics
item.description: Rather than compromise and slow down the decision-making process, business leaders need real-time data to inform and complement their gut feel
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Navigating the rising costs of AI inference in the era of large-scale applications
item.description: Exploring how organizations balance cost considerations while satisfying demands for speed and scale.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump faces crypto world backlash after launch of meme coin
item.description: President Trump is facing backlash after he and first lady Melania Trump launched two cryptocurrency tokens just days before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5090 and 5080 GPU stock shortage rumors are piling high – and now there’s a suggestion the RTX 5070 could be delayed
item.description: Fresh Nvidia next-gen GPU fears emerge: shaky stock, pricing worries, and a claim the RTX 5070 is delayed until March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Donald Trump’s executive order puts Santa Cruz EV charging grant in jeopardy
item.description: Reps. Zoe Lofgren and Jimmy Panetta announced they had helped secure more than $14.3 million from the U.S. Department of Transportation that was expected to go toward the installation of electric vehicle charging ports at 44 sites across Santa Cruz and Monterey counties.
item.dc:creator: Pk Hattis, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump pardons Silk Road dark web market creator Ross Ulbricht
item.description: President Trump signs a “full and unconditional” pardon for Silk Road creator Ross Ulbricht, who had received two life sentences.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: John Malkovich and The Bear’s Ayo Edebiri get swept up in a pop music cult in first trailer for A24’s Opus – and it's gone straight to the top of my watch list
item.description: A24 has dropped a thrilling first trailer for music horror Opus, and it's gone straight to the top of my 2025 watchlist. Watch the first full trailer here.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Free updates are coming to Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 after launch, including the ability to get a haircut
item.description: New Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 post launch roadmap details the expansion content and free updates coming to the game.
item.dc:creator: dash.wood@futurenet.com (Dashiell Wood), TechRadar

item.title: 5 practical tips for emerging IT leaders
item.description: When they announced my name on stage at CIO Summit and Awards 2024 as the winner for Emerging ICT Leader of the Year award, my profound disbelief and shock flared up my impostor syndrome one more time as I walked up the stage.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump plays dealmaker as questions surround TikTok
item.description: President Trump's efforts to force TikTok's Chinese parent company to sell the popular app, all while keeping it available to U.S. users despite Sunday’s ban, raise a slew of legal and practical questions about the platform's future.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: For the first time ever, I wish Google would act more like Amazon
item.description: Fair warning: This isn’t your average article about what’s happening with all the newfangled AI hullabaloo in this weird and wild world of ours.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Herencia, propósito y creatividad confluyen sobre un manto tecnológico en los irrepetibles UMusic Hotels
item.description: Madrid tiene alma, no solo porque goza de personalidad y estilo propio, sino porque ahora, además, también suena diferente. Y es que desde que la cadena hotelera UMusic Hotels desembarcara en la capital, la música ha pasado a servir de hilo conductor a la historia que aquí escribe cada huésped.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump shrugs off billions made through meme coin launch
item.description: President Trump shrugged off the news that he made billions of dollars through the launch of his new meme crypto coin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces up to $500 billion investment to build AI infrastructure
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday announced the creation of a new project to invest up to $500 billion in building the infrastructure needed for artificial intelligence (AI) development over his second term.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Extreme cold and snow across the South isn't a threat to most native plants and animals, experts say
item.description: A deep freeze and rare snow have brought most things to a standstill from parts of Texas to Florida, but experts say wildlife natives like cypress trees and prairie plants, and cold-blooded gators and snakes, should be alright.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple Asked to Rename Gulf of Mexico
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw urges Apple to rename the Gulf of Mexico on its mapping platform following President Trump's executive order declaring it the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 10 tech upgrades to save your time, privacy and money this year
item.description: Today's tech can help make life so much easier — if you know how to use it. Here are a few tips on the quickest and best ways to make your phone, laptop and other devices work for you.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Today’s AI models have a poor grasp of world history
item.description: Today’s AI models do a poor job of providing accurate information about world history, according to a new report from the Austrian research institute Complexity Science Hub (CSH).
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple will add AI Mail tools to Macs and iPads this spring
item.description: Apple is about to improve its email application, Mail, on iPads and Macs. Both platforms will soon gain the same Apple Intelligence email summary and prioritization systems already available on iPhones — though not every iPhone user seems to have embraced the new systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Army testing approach to ease communication with allies
item.description: Col. Heath Giesecke, the director of the Army’s Enterprise Cloud Management Agency, said a cloud-based commercial app is helping close a communication gap. The post Army testing approach to ease communication with allies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Cloud infrastructure spending more than doubles in the third quarter of 2024
item.description: Spending on compute and storage infrastructure for cloud deployments has surged to unprecedented heights, with 115.3% year-over-year increase in the third quarter of 2024.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025’s first Patch Tuesday: 159 patches, including several zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft began 2025 with a hefty patch release this month, addressing eight zero-days with 159 patches for Windows, Microsoft Office and Visual Studio. Both Windows and Microsoft Office have “Patch Now” recommendations (with no browser or Exchange patches) for January.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump's inauguration will usher in a crypto-friendly administration, and with it, new state policies
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — The bitcoin-friendly administration of President-elect Donald Trump and an expanding lobbying effort in statehouses could push states to become more open to crypto and lead public pension funds and treasuries to buy into it.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Melania meme coin launches following Trump's new cryptocurrency
item.description: The website for the coin describes “Melania Memes” as “fungible crypto assets created and tracked on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: How TikTok grew from a fun app for teens into a potential national security threat
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — If it feels like TikTok has been around forever, that's probably because it has, at least if you're measuring via internet time.
item.dc:creator: DAVID HAMILTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: “The 90-day extension is something that will be most likely done, because it's appropriate," Trump said in a call with NBC News.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Fake job interview emails installing hidden cryptocurrency mining malware
item.description: Scammers have a new trick where they pretend to be job recruiters to plant malicious apps on your devices. It all starts with a deceptive email.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Secure AI? Dream on, says AI red team
item.description: The group responsible for red teaming of over 100 generative AI products at Microsoft has concluded that the work of building safe and secure AI systems will never be complete.
item.dc:creator: Info World
